title: Git Mental Model, Setup, Help, and Repository Safety · Graph Lens: Help Version Safety
summary: Adopt an unfamiliar repository without overwriting identity, hooks, ignored files, or team policy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git help version safety increment in a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.
Set and verify author identity, default branch, editor, pager, and line-ending policy without changing unrelated scopes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Help Version Safety
steps.instruction:
theory case — Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 5.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Help Version Safety contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.

content.text: Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 5.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.

steps.title: Bound Help Version Safety
steps.instruction:
theory case — Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 6.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Help Version Safety application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.

content.text: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 5.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it. A defensible solution applies Help Version Safety, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook.

content.code:
# Evidence: changed Git case 3
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A command from a current web page behaves identically on every installed Git version. This fails the Help Version Safety contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Repair the Help Version Safety defect
steps.instruction:
theory case — Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 4.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.

A teammate acts on this belief: “A command from a current web page behaves identically on every installed Git version.” Which repair addresses the cause?

steps.title: Build Help Version Safety evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-help-version-safety-1qrcso" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Help Version Safety into observable performance evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.
